_comment: The bar menu Ballmer recommends from. Each drink has a vibe score (1-10) reflecting its character for a late-night coding session: balance of enjoyability, complexity, and appropriateness to the Ballmer Peak context. High vibe = sophisticated and enjoyable; low vibe = functional-at-best or disastrous-at-worst.
_ethanol_formula: per ingredient: oz * 29.5735 ml/oz * abv * 0.789 g/ml; summed per drink

drinks.name: Lager (12 oz)
drinks.notes: reliable, sessionable, no surprises

drinks.name: IPA (12 oz)
drinks.notes: craft credibility, hoppy complexity

drinks.name: Red wine (5 oz)
drinks.notes: contemplative, slow-sipping energy

drinks.name: White wine (5 oz)
drinks.notes: crisp, clean, keeps you sharp

drinks.name: Sparkling (5 oz)
drinks.notes: celebratory, effervescent, light on ethanol

drinks.name: Whiskey, neat (2 oz)
drinks.notes: no-nonsense, maximum ethanol efficiency, programmer's handshake

drinks.name: Whiskey, rocks (2 oz)
drinks.notes: same ethanol as neat, slightly more casual

drinks.name: Double whiskey, rocks (4 oz)
drinks.notes: the serious pour — twice the ethanol, handle with care

drinks.name: Vodka soda
drinks.notes: functional, low-calorie, zero personality

drinks.name: Gin & tonic
drinks.notes: botanical, crisp, the de facto developer drink

drinks.name: Margarita
drinks.notes: tequila + triple sec + lime, festive and sharp

drinks.name: Manhattan
drinks.notes: rye + sweet vermouth, the Wall Street coder's drink

drinks.name: Negroni
drinks.notes: equal parts gin/Campari/vermouth, bittersweet complexity

drinks.name: Dry martini
drinks.notes: gin-forward, zero dilution, peak Ballmer Peak energy

drinks.name: Old fashioned
drinks.notes: bourbon + sugar + bitters, the classic for a reason

drinks.name: Mojito
drinks.notes: white rum + lime + soda + mint, refreshing and alert

drinks.name: Cosmopolitan
drinks.notes: vodka + triple sec + cranberry, fun but dated

drinks.name: Espresso martini
drinks.notes: vodka + coffee liqueur + espresso, dual-purpose productivity hack

drinks.name: Aperol spritz
drinks.notes: low-ABV aperitivo, good for a slow climb

drinks.name: Long Island iced tea
drinks.notes: four spirits at once — blows past the ceiling, no finesse

drinks.name: Water / soda (NA)
drinks.notes: the soft-nudge option — only when past ceiling
